category_title: Students with low academic achievement engaging in reflective knowledge-building discourse
minimal_context: The student is analyzing their Knowledge Connection Analyzer data on the Knowledge Forum to evaluate their collaboration and plan improvements.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Please look at your KCA data and the task sheet to analyze the question 'Are we a community that collaborates?'

STUDENT: I can see my deficiencies. For example, reading others' notes, I have read many notes, the percentage is 99% which is pretty high.

TUTOR: What do the other percentages show?

STUDENT: When I look at built-on to others' notes, the percentage is only about 20%, which is pretty low. My notes have been read a lot, but built on less.

TUTOR: What do you think you should do next?

STUDENT: I need to communicate more with peers, but my notes lack thought-provoking questions. I am not happy with the quality of my notes, and I am afraid that my point or idea is not good enough.

options.A: Rearrange the area so the computers face back to back and away from the general student body, and move the KCA task sheets and reflection journals to the middle of the classroom.
options.B: Remain available without forcing interaction, first simply sitting near the student during the KCA analysis and then speaking quietly and gently touching them on the shoulder.
options.C: Persuade other students to allow the student to join their Knowledge Forum discussions, and keep a close eye on the dynamics of the online discourse.
options.D: Guide the student to write high-quality notes by referencing knowledge-building principles, and prompt them to contribute more notes through reflection on the existing assessment tools in Knowledge Forum.
